entry.title: 利用 SAP 的企业自动化处理销售线索到收款流程
entry.content:
本文为翻译文，原文链接Leverage Enterprise Automation with SAP for Lead-to-Cash processes，原文作者Manuel_Namyslo。

简介

在现代业务的动态架构中，企业面临着将潜在销售线索高效转化为收入的挑战。销售线索到现金流程在此过程中起着关键作用，包括从销售线索生成到现金收款的各个阶段。销售、营销和财务职能的无缝集成对于持续的业务增长和成功至关重要。现代销售线索到收款流程包括以下步骤：

 

通过优先考虑从销售线索生成到现金收款的无缝活动流，企业能够优化资源，提升客户体验，并在各自行业中扮演敏捷且响应迅速的企业。但是，许多企业都面临着从销售线索到现金的难题，这与流程管理不佳密切相关：

 

  • 客户旅程不明确
  • 销售线索跟踪和管理流程不足
  • 销售团队与营销团队之间沟通不畅
  • 流程期间的数据捕获不足
  • 数据质量问题
  • 难以准确预测销售目标
  • 客户启用周期较长
  • 延迟开票付款或收款问题

 

在本博文中，我想向您展示如何利用企业自动化来克服销售线索到现金领域的低效流程、重复性任务和不良的流程管理。但首先，什么是 SAP 的企业自动化？

 

什么是企业自动化？

借助 SAP 的企业自动化功能，我们的客户能够利用 SAP Integration Suite、SAP Signavio 和 SAP Build 等一流的工具，快速、轻松地洞察业务流程，并利用合适的工具实现自动化，实现出色的业务成果。这些工具与现有业务应用程序无缝协作，不会增加复杂性：

 

  • SAP Integration Suite 是一款集成平台即服务 (iPaaS)，具有数千个与 SAP 和非 SAP 应用程序的预构建集成、API 和连接器。它降低了企业预置和多云环境的复杂性，可帮助您实现优化的端到端业务流程。
  • SAP Signavio 提供基于 200,000 个行业基准和最佳实践的业务流程可见性、潜在瓶颈、根本原因分析以及改进建议。深入了解效率低下有助于组织优化和自动化流程。
  • SAP Build 是一款低代码解决方案，可以轻松拖放创建自动化和应用。它包括面向 SAP 和非 SAP 系统的预构建自动化和连接器。业务专家可以使用 SAP Build 快速解决流程低效问题。

 

 

 

销售线索到收款的预构建内容

从 SAP 的企业自动化开始的最简单方法是使用预构建内容。内容提供技术部件，您可以实施这些部件，以及直接来自 SAP 开发团队和合作伙伴的最佳实践和建议。您可以在此处找到我们的内容：

SAP Build – 预构建内容

在此，您可以找到一些专为优化从线索到收款而设计的内容示例：

通过 Excel 自动创建销售订单

使用参考创建产品主数据

使用灵活工作流的销售订单审批扩展示例

线索到订单的智能工作流

使用 Excel 通过电子邮件自动创建销售订单

创建客户物料信息记录

批量维护销售合同

根据非结构化数据自动创建销售订单请求

项目经理项目创建

项目活动触发器

 

将此内容用于您的优势 - 因为它不仅为您提供可在 SAP Build Lobby 中部署的技术部件，还为您提供来自开发团队和合作伙伴且经认证可上载 SAP Build 内容的建议和最佳实践。此外，我们还确保定期更新和维护内容。

 

用例

让我们用一个用例来具体了解一下！销售订单处理是“销售线索到现金”领域的常见场景。它可以包括用户体验和流程执行方面的特定需求。例如，数据验证的层次审批步骤或用于分类折扣规则或客户细分的复杂业务规则。或将销售订单创建连接到多个记录系统的特定集成要求。在此，您可以查看场景外观的概览：

我们建议从 SAP Signavio Process Insights 开始。在此，您可以查看流程架构并评估端到端场景，如销售线索到收款、寻源到付款和记录到报表。

根据你的数据发现卓越实践和改进建议，并对标行业标准。在这里，你可以看到“销售线索到收款”业务线中出现的低效场景，这些场景具有较高的自动化潜力。您还可以直接获得 SAP Business Accelerator Hub 中预构建内容的建议：

 

在 SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 中，您可以执行流程挖掘以深入了解整个销售线索到收款流程，并深入了解销售订单凭证创建的每个流程变式。

发现不同的流变化并确定需要改进的瓶颈，例如销售订单凭证的手动处理：

 

 

基于 BPMN 2.0 可视化流程，并查看 SAP S/4HANA 中订单创建的所有必要步骤。您还可以与利益相关方协作，以确定改进领域并确定其优先级。

 

 

发现 SAP Signavio 的流程效率低下后，您可以跳转到 SAP Build 以生成自定义扩展。在这种情况下，我们利用 SAP Build Apps 创建可定制的移动应用，以增强销售订单处理。每个销售代表都可以使用该自定义应用，将新销售订单输入到记录系统中。

为此，我们利用基于低代码的设计时间，并在开发流程中包括多个利益相关方：

 

 

 

还可以使用流程自动化扩展应用程序，以协调工作流场景和 Robotic Process Automation (RPA)。

通过可定制的审批步骤、业务规则、机器学习和与 SAP 和非 SAP 系统的本机连接，在 SAP S/4HANA 中执行销售订单创建流程：

 

 

 

在下一个屏幕截图中，您可以看到用于检查和审批销售订单凭证的嵌入式表单构建器。然后，审批人可以与 SAP Task Center 中的流程任务进行交互，例如批准或拒绝销售订单凭证。

通过此工具，您可以利用直观的拖放功能以及预先开发的 UI 元素：

 

除本机连接器外，您还可以使用表面自动化轻松集成到 SAP 和非 SAP 系统。

这是使用经典 RPA 功能（如屏幕录制和屏幕捕获）自动执行重复和手动任务的常用方法。在这种情况下，我们可以将新的销售订单凭证输入到 SAP S/4HANA 或将交易数据或主数据复制到系统中：

在开发项目中，您会很快注意到的一点是，您无法实现任何无法集成的自动化。尽管 SAP Build 允许您连接到 SAP 和非 SAP 系统，但 SAP Integration Suite 可以将连接引入完全不同的级别。

使用此工具，您可以生成自己的集成流，以将流程连接到异构 IT 架构中的任意类型的应用程序。 利用预构建内容，通过高级值映射和消息转换在第三方应用程序中复制销售订单凭证：

 

 

结论

 

Enterprise Automation 目前是 SAP 的最热门主题，它允许您在异构环境中集成应用程序、发现低效环节和自动化流程。投资这项计划必将带来长期的业务成果，并采用完善的方法自动运行和管理企业内部的自动化流程。

这只是如何在“销售线索到收款”上下文中使用这些技术的示例。请记住，此框架具有高度适应性，可用于任何业务范围、行业或用例。因此，请务必查看我们的其他资料、赋能和文档，开启 SAP 企业自动化之旅：

 

  • 请访问 sap.com/enterprise-automation
  • 查看 SAP Build Store 中的预构建集成和自动化内容
  • 查看《教程导航器》中的“实践”材料和说明

 

当然，如果你想快速开启低代码开发之旅，加入我们的 SAP Builders 社区，开始学习 SAP Build 解决方案！

entry.title: 利用 SAP 的企业自动化实现记录到报告流程
entry.content:
本文为翻译文，原文链接Leverage Enterprise Automation with SAP for Record-to-Report processes，原文作者Manuel_Namyslo。

 

简介

记录到报告是一个重要的财务流程，对寻求透明度、问责制和战略决策的组织来说至关重要。此综合流程涉及财务交易的记录、处理和报告，最终导致创建准确可靠的财务报表。以下是汇总记录到报告流程的主要步骤和活动：

 

总之，记录到报告流程是财务管理的支柱，为组织成功奠定了基础。其影响不仅限于合规性，还涉及战略决策、风险管理和利益相关方关系。如果执行不当，则无法促进弹性、敏捷和透明的财务框架，并导致企业组织面临以下难题：  

 

  • 订单处理延迟
  • 客户信息不完整或不准确
  • 库存管理挑战
  • 与客户沟通效率低下
  • 信用和开票问题
  • 无效订单履行
  • 发票争议和核算问题
  • 付款处理挑战
  • 客户服务和支持问题
  • 法规合规性问题

 

在本博文中，我们将向您展示如何利用 SAP 的企业自动化来管理和优化订单到收款流程。但首先，什么是 SAP 的企业自动化？

 

什么是企业自动化？

借助 SAP 的企业自动化功能，我们的客户能够利用 SAP Integration Suite、SAP Signavio 和 SAP Build 等一流的工具，快速、轻松地洞察业务流程，并利用合适的工具实现自动化，实现出色的业务成果。这些工具与现有业务应用程序无缝协作，不会增加复杂性。

 

  • SAP Integration Suite 是一款集成平台即服务 (iPaaS)，具有数千个与 SAP 和非 SAP 应用程序的预构建集成、API 和连接器。它降低了企业预置和多云环境的复杂性，可帮助您实现优化的端到端业务流程。

 

  • SAP Signavio 提供基于 200,000 个行业基准和最佳实践的业务流程可见性、潜在瓶颈、根本原因分析以及改进建议。深入了解效率低下有助于组织优化和自动化流程。

 

  • SAP Build 是一款低代码解决方案，可以轻松拖放创建自动化和应用。它包括面向 SAP 和非 SAP 系统的预构建自动化和连接器。业务专家可以使用 SAP Build 快速解决流程低效问题。

 

 

 

 

记录到报告的预构建内容：

从 SAP 的企业自动化开始的最简单方法是使用预构建内容。预构建内容提供技术部件，您可以实施这些部件，以及直接来自 SAP 开发团队和合作伙伴的最佳实践和建议。您可以在此处找到我们的内容：

SAP Build – 预构建内容

在此，您可以找到一些专为优化记录到报告而设计的内容示例：

从电子邮件提取供应商发票文件并上载到 SAP Central Invoice Management

总账科目条目的建议重新分类

在不影响财务过账的情况下，批量更新总账日记账分录明细

批量更新资产主数据

批量上载付款通知

自动上载总账分录

批量上传银行对账单文件

自动批量上载总账日记账分录

自动收货/收发票清算

应计过账的批量自动化

总账主记录创建流程

创建收货 (BAPI)

日记账分录过账审批

催款运行 - F150 (ECC)

 

将此内容转化为您的优势，因为它不仅为您提供可在 SAP Build Lobby 中部署的技术部件，还为您提供来自开发团队和合作伙伴且经认证可上载 SAP Build 内容的建议和最佳实践。此外，我们还确保定期更新和维护内容。

 

用例

让我们通过真实用例深入了解这个主题！如果没有简化和自动化过账新总账科目等财务流程，则可能需要手动和重复活动。尤其是当业务组织需要特定流程流来满足合规性和法规要求时。这包括用于审批人确定的业务规则、总账科目的不同分类或各种条件或阈值（也需要考虑）。

在此，您可以看到 S/4HANA 中基于审批的总账科目过账的外观：

我们建议从 SAP Signavio Process Insights 开始，以获取流程架构和最大自动化潜力的初始概览。获取直接改进建议，了解记录到报告方面的预构建内容和卓越实践。

在此，您可以利用 Robotic Process Automation 机器人和工作流流程来改进财务流程。所有建议均在 Signavio 中列出，如屏幕截图所示：

在 SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 中启动调查，以进一步了解财务关账、日记账分录过账或在总账中处理新记录等场景。

生成有关流程绩效指标（如平均周期时间或流程相关性）的分析仪表盘。

 

为了过账新的总账科目，您可以为业务用户提供中央访问点，以访问 SAP 和非 SAP 源中的应用程序和流程。此中央访问点随 SAP Build Work Zone（标准版）提供。

业务用户可以直接从主页启动创建总账科目的流程。

 

在此，您可以查看直观的表单应用程序，以允许业务用户将流程实例作为自助服务触发。 

在这种情况下，您可以启动审批工作流以在 SAP S/4HANA 中创建新总账科目。

 

在流程逻辑中，您可以引入审批人并处理利益相关方以验证关键数据并确保合规性。 

现在，业务用户可以丰富附加信息并相应地审批账户。

 

使用 SAP Build Process Automation 开发具有预构建流程活动的自定义流程，可以在图形布局设计器中进行配置。

设计单个审批工作流并处理用于过账总账科目的业务规则。

 

项目用于生产后，您可以检查每个流程实例的状态并检查关键业务数据。

申请者可以检查已发送审批的新客户的状态。

 

连接到各种后端系统，以进行数据验证、数据转换和实体映射。

在此，您还可以利用连接器集合在审批表单中检索总账科目编号范围详细信息。

 

 

结论

Enterprise Automation 目前是 SAP 的最热门主题，它允许您在异构环境中集成应用程序、发现低效环节和自动化流程。投资这项计划必将带来长期的业务成果，并采用完善的方法自动运行和管理企业内部的自动化流程。

这只是如何在记录到报告上下文中使用这些技术的示例。请记住，此框架具有高度适应性，可用于任何业务范围、行业或用例。因此，请务必查看我们的其他资料、赋能和文档，开启 SAP 企业自动化之旅：

 

  • 请访问 sap.com/enterprise-automation
  • 查看 SAP Build Store 中的预构建集成和自动化内容
  • 查看《教程导航器》中的“实践”材料和说明

entry.title: Three Ways to Deliver End-to-End Data and AI Governance with SAP and Collibra
entry.content:
In today’s data and AI-driven world, businesses have recognized the untapped potential of their enterprise data but still struggle to take control of the data locked within their complex data environments. With many data silos, numerous applications, and constantly growing data volumes, organizations struggle to understand their data and provide users with reliable access to it, all while maintaining compliance and transparency.

This is why SAP has partnered with Collibra: SAP supplies valuable business data through SAP applications and the business data fabric, while Collibra is a recognized leader in enterprise data governance. SAP Datasphere and Collibra together support a data strategy that emphasizes governance for data and AI, unlocking an organization's enterprise data potential.

 

SAP and Collibra for Trusted Data and AI

Collibra is essential to SAP's strategy for an open data ecosystem and plays a crucial role in providing comprehensive data and AI governance for the business data fabric. A business data fabric is a data management architecture that is built over underlying data landscapes and works by connecting, managing, and governing data across different systems and applications to provide a centralized view and seamless access to data without duplication. 

SAP’s partnership with Collibra began in early 2023 for end-to-end data governance, and was recently expanded to address the AI governance needs of our customers with Collibra AI Governance. SAP aims to seamlessly integrate AI into all business processes while also assisting customers in creating their own customized AI solutions using crucial business data from SAP applications. Collibra Data Intelligence Platform is an SAP Endorsed app available in the SAP Store and offers data cataloging, governance, data quality, privacy, and AI governance capabilities.

Together, SAP with Collibra enable organizations to deliver trusted data to every data consumer and drive trustworthy AI across the data landscape.

 

Unlocking the Potential of your Data

Businesses gather increasing amounts of data every day. The sheer volume and the number of applications involved make it challenging to track the types of data collected throughout the organization, let alone manage compliance and understand the value it may offer.

There are three ways for customers to start unlocking the potential of their data with SAP and Collibra.

1. Take control of your data landscape with end-to-end data governance

The potential of data is limitless, and advancements focused on data will only continue to speed up with the rise of AI. It’s crucial for organizations to take control of their data environments now, to pave the way for adopting new technologies in the future. These technologies have the potential to transform customer experiences, streamline business processes, and create new business opportunities.

The Collibra Data Intelligence Platform is a comprehensive governance solution with cataloging, governance, data quality, privacy, and AI governance capabilities. The tools provided for data discovery, cataloging, and metadata management allow organizations to effortlessly maintain a business-friendly, searchable catalog of data and analytics assets across the enterprise.

With automated data quality capabilities, data can be continuously assessed and monitored to identify and address issues like data inaccuracies and inconsistencies. This helps to ensure that data-driven business decisions are based on reliable and high-quality data.

Collibra promotes a data-driven mindset and enhances data literacy by providing a standardized business glossary and collaborative features that facilitate communication among various data stakeholders like data stewards, business users, and IT professionals.

Finally, the platform handles data risk and compliance, ensuring businesses follow data protection regulations like GDPR, CCPA, and HIPAA. Data privacy has become increasingly important as more information is shared and stored digitally and used for transformative initiatives like Generative AI.

With Collibra, businesses can be in control of their data landscape, which is essential for any data-driven organization to succeed and will also help pave the way for AI-driven innovations.

2. Make data easier to find, understand, and trust across the organization

Unlocking your data's potential begins with understanding the data you already have and enabling your users to easily discover, understand, and trust the data to drive better business outcomes. For example, your users could combine customer data from marketing, sales, and support to create 360-degree customer views – but only if they can find and trust the data they need.

Collibra Data Catalog is part of the Collibra Data Intelligence Platform and enables organizations to manage, discover, and govern their data and analytics assets effectively. It can scan and profile data and analytics assets from 100+ sources (data warehouses, BI tools, applications, etc.) and create a centralized catalog that includes metadata with rich technical, business and operational context like descriptions, owner, source, business terms and more. It also offers capabilities such as data lineage, impact analysis, and a standardized business glossary that can increase data literacy within an organization. With Collibra Data Catalog, users can easily find and trust the data they need to make better data-driven decisions.

SAP Datasphere is the foundation for a business data fabric and provides a unified experience for data integration, data cataloging, semantic modeling, data warehousing, data federation, and data virtualization. SAP Datasphere catalog is the metadata management solution for SAP applications. It collects and organizes metadata and assets from SAP sources, and currently supports SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Datasphere, and SAP Datasphere BW Bridge assets. In the roadmap, SAP Datasphere catalog will expand its support to include metadata from additional SAP sources including SAP BW/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP HANA Cloud. The catalog helps you stay connected with all the SAP data and analytics assets throughout your business, providing a continuous flow of information to assist you in monitoring your business performance.

Figure 1: SAP and non-SAP metadata collected and organized in Collibra Data Intelligence Platform

SAP and Collibra are working closely together to build deep integration that allows detailed metadata and lineage from SAP sources to be shared with Collibra via SAP Datasphere, enabling an end-to-end data catalog across SAP and non-SAP sources. The initial integration will be become available in the Q3 2024 release of Collibra Data Intelligence Platform with support for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere assets. 

Going back to the earlier example, users can use Collibra to find sales, customer and product support data from SAP sources, consumer data from Google, and other useful third-party data. The same users can then use SAP Datasphere to gain access to these data products and harmonize the data in a semantically rich analytical model without duplication to form 360-degree views of their customers.

More opportunities within your data are waiting to be uncovered with the combination of SAP Datasphere and Collibra.

3. Build AI with governance best practices to maximize results and reduce risk

The rise of AI and its growing role in automating customer experiences, tasks, and decisions highlights the importance of trusted data and the need to extend governance to AI. AI can unleash the potential of your data, but it requires careful governance and monitoring. This includes supervising how the data is used in AI, ensuring ethical handling of the data, complying with privacy and regulatory rules, and addressing other relevant aspects. For instance, insufficient understanding and insight into the data powering AI can result in biased AI, resulting in costly consequences such as legal action and damage to a company's reputation. An AI governance framework can be implemented with Collibra AI Governance to assist in maximizing results while steering clear of the risks and costs often linked with AI.

 

Video: Daniel Yu, SVP of SAP Data & Analytics with Laura Sellers, Chief Product Owner of Collibra

SAP, with collaboration from Collibra, will address the data and AI governance needs of joint customers through Collibra AI Governance. Integrations with SAP Datasphere, SAP HANA Cloud, and SAP Business AI solutions will be built to ensure the development of AI that is relevant, reliable and responsible.

 

Learn More about SAP and Collibra

SAP’s partnership with Collibra will continue to expand and grow stronger as the interest and demand for data and AI governance increases.

To learn more about how SAP and Collibra work together, check out the following:

  • Register for our upcoming webinar - “Enhance your data strategy with Collibra and SAP Datasphere” July 24, 2024 10:00 AM – 11:00 AM ET
  • Learn more about the SAP and Collibra Partnership
  • Take a guided basic trial of SAP Datasphere
  • Take a tour of Collibra Data Intelligence Platform

entry.title: Consume native script plugin for playing audio in the SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK)
entry.content:
The SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) empowers developers to create cross-platform mobile applications with ease. However, there are times when the standard features of MDK need to be extended to incorporate native functionalities such as audio playback. This is where NativeScript plugins come into play. In this blog post, we will explore how to consume a NativeScript plugin for playing audio/sound within an MDK application on both Android and iOS platforms.

Prerequisites

Before we dive into the implementation, ensure you have the following prerequisites:

  1. SAP Mobile Services Account: Access to SAP Mobile Services and a configured MDK app.
  2. NativeScript CLI: Installed on your development machine.
  3. Base MDK Project: Set up the base MDK project in Visual Studio Code.

Step 1: Adding the NativeScript Audio Plugin

To add the NativeScript plugin for audio playback in an MDK project, follow these steps:

  • Install Plugin: Run this command in the terminal within your .mdkproject folder:

 

npm i nativescript-audio

 

  • Update MDKProject.json: Add the plugin to the Externals, NSPlugins, and NPMPlugins sections: 

 

{
  "AppDisplayName": "Base MDK Project",
  "AppName": "BaseMDKProject",
  "AppVersion": "1.0.0",
  "BundleID": "com.sap.csd.mdk.basemdkproject",
  "Externals": ["nativescript-audio"],
  "NSPlugins": ["nativescript-audio"],
  "NPMPlugins": ["nativescript-audio"],
  "UrlScheme": "basemdkproject",
}

 

  • Update settings.json: Add the plugin to mdk.bundlerExternals in the settings.json file in Visual Studio Code. Here is the screenshot for reference:

Step 2: Integrating the Audio Functionality to MDK

To expose the audio playback functionality to your MDK app, you need to create an MDK rule that interacts with the NativeScript plugin code. Create a new rule file in your MDK project, e.g., OnPressPlaySound.js.

 

import { TNSPlayer } from 'nativescript-audio';
const fs = require('@nativescript/core/file-system');

/**
 * Play beep sound
 */
export default function OnPressPlaySound(clientAPI) {
  const player = new TNSPlayer();
  if (clientAPI.nativescript.platformModule.isIOS) { // For iOS
    const folder = fs.knownFolders.currentApp();
    const file = folder._path.slice(0, -3) + 'audio/beep.mp3';
    player.debug = true; // set true to enable TNSPlayer console logs for debugging.
    player.initFromFile({
        audioFile: file, // ~ = app directory
        loop: false,
      }).then(() => {
        player.getAudioTrackDuration().then((duration) => {
          console.log(`song duration:`, duration);
          player.play();
        });
      });
  } else if (clientAPI.nativescript.platformModule.isAndroid) { // For Android
    const logger = clientAPI.getLogger();
    console.log('Current Log Level: ' + logger.getLevel());
    player.playFromFile({
      audioFile: '~/audio/beep.mp3',
      loop: false,
    }).then(() => {
      console.log('[Audio Player] Done');
    }).catch((err) => {
      console.log('[Audio Player] Error: ' + err);
    });
  }
}

 

Step 3: Importing and using audio playback functionality

To utilize the audio playback functionality defined in OnPressPlaySound.js across different rules in your MDK application, follow these steps:

In the example below, consider the name of the rule as SoundCheck.js.

  • Importing OnPressPlaySound Function

In your SoundCheck.js rule file, import the OnPressPlaySound function from OnPressPlaySound.js.

 

import OnPressPlaySound from './OnPressPlaySound';

 

  • Calling the Audio Playback Function

Wherever audio playback is required within the SoundCheck.js rule, call the imported OnPressPlaySound function.

 

export default function SoundCheck(clientAPI) {
    // Example usage of OnPressPlaySound
    OnPressPlaySound(clientAPI);

    // Additional logic specific to SoundCheck rule
    // ...
}

 

The overall SoundCheck.js would look like this:

 

import OnPressPlaySound from './OnPressPlaySound';

/**
 * Method to check audio playback
 */
export default function SoundCheck(clientAPI) {
    // Example usage of OnPressPlaySound
    OnPressPlaySound(clientAPI);

    // Additional logic specific to SoundCheck rule
    // ...
}

 

To utilize the audio playback functionality defined in OnPressPlaySound.js directly from a page in your MDK application, follow these steps:

In the example below, consider a page which has a toolbar item.

  • Directly add the Rule in the OnPress action of a Toolbar item

 

"ToolBar": {
	"Items": [
		{
			"Position": "Right",
			"_Type": "Control.Type.ToolbarItem",
			"_Name": "ToolbarItem",
			"Caption": "Play audio",
			"Enabled": true,
			"Visible": true,
			"Clickable": true,
			"Style": "",
			"OnPress": "/BaseMDKProject/Rules/OnPressPlaySound.js"
		}
	]
}

 

Step 4: Add resource file to the project

To play sound in your MDK application, you need to include the audio file (e.g., beep.mp3) within your project. Follow these steps to ensure the audio file is correctly placed:

Important Considerations

Binary files such as .mp3 cannot be placed arbitrarily within the MDK project. For instance, placing them inside the metadata folder can lead to project build failures. To avoid such issues, the audio files must be located in a specific directory structure within your project.

Directory Structure

Create the App_Resources folder within your .mdkproject and organize the audio files as shown below:

Summary of Directory Structure

PlatformPath
AndroidApp_Resources/Android/src/main/assets/app/audio/beep.mp3
iOSApp_Resources/iOS/src/beep.mp3

 

Step 5: Deploying and Testing

Deploy your MDK app to your desired environment. Make sure to test the audio playback functionality on both Android and iOS devices to ensure compatibility and performance.

 

tns run android
tns run ios

 

Step 6: Distribution of the MDK application

A customized mobile development client is required for distributing this application on Android and iOS platforms. For more details on how to generate the customized MDK client refer to step 4 in the tutorial: Build Your Mobile Development Kit Client Using Cloud Build Service

Conclusion

Integrating NativeScript plugins into your SAP MDK application allows you to extend the functionality of your mobile apps with native capabilities. By following the steps outlined in this blog post, you can seamlessly integrate audio playback into your MDK app using the nativescript-audio plugin. This approach not only enhances the user experience but also leverages the power of NativeScript to bridge the gap between standard MDK features and native device functionalities.

entry.title: Grounding is All You Need
entry.content:
Hey everyone,

Following my last blog post (SAPPHIRE 2024 – My impressions and vision on what is ahead for us), I want to dive into something crucial that SAP needs to address ASAP: Groundings. Let me explain why this is so important.

 

What Are Groundings?

In the world of Generative AI (GenAI), one of the key building blocks is Groundings, also known as knowledge bases. Groundings enable a technique called RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) to expand the knowledge of a Large Language Model (LLM). Here’s why this matters:

  • LLM Data Cutoff: LLMs have a cutoff date for the data they can access. They know a lot up to a certain date, but not beyond.
  • Limited Knowledge on Private Data: LLMs might lack information on personal or company-specific data.
  • Specific Application Data: Sometimes, you need to add very specific application data that LLMs don’t know.

 

With RAG, we query vector databases to pre-select semantically related information and pass it to the LLM for additional context. This process is hugely beneficial.

 

The Need for SAP to Build Groundings

 

While we’re all working on building our own groundings, there are some that SAP should create and publish. Here are the most pressing ones I can think of:

 

  1. HELP.SAP.COM: Groundings on help.sap would allow us to create chatbots that can easily answer user queries. This is likely a foundation grounding of Joule, but we all need this. Handling different versions and products is a challenge, but it’s workable. Groundings should be created by product/version.
  2. SAP Training Documentation: This is more complex due to the variety of data types, but incredibly valuable. It extends far beyond what HELP.SAP covers.
  3. ABAP (or Other Technical) Documentation: While SAP has mentioned working on a fine-tuned LLM version, a grounding with numerous referenceable examples would greatly improve quality. Current LLM-generated ABAP examples often display questionable examples.
  4. SAP API Documentation: Detailed explanations on the function and usage of all SAP APIs would be an incredible addition for functional, ABAP, and integration developers. "Is there an API to perform X?" "How do I call this particular API?"
  5. S/4 Data Model: Though more complex to structure, a grounding with all SAP tables, their descriptions, and relations to supporting tables would be beneficial. Utilizing the CDS views repository could be a starting point. "How do I determine delivery status for sales order items?"
  6. COMMUNITY.SAP.COM: There’s a wealth of great content in blogs and forum conversations that could be turned into groundings.
  7. SAP Support Tickets: While sensitive, anonymized support tickets could be made available to customers and partners, significantly reducing support costs across the ecosystem. I’d be willing to pay for that.

How SAP Can Make Groundings Available

 

SAP could build and offer these groundings in several ways:

  • For Free: Some of it could be offered for free. 🙂
  • GenAI Hub: Groundings could be integrated into the GenAI Hub, increasing the platform’s value.
  • Licensed Access: Groundings could be licensed and charged based on access, allowing consumption anywhere.

 

Conclusion

These are just a few examples of the groundings SAP could develop to enhance our tools and workflows. It’s time for SAP to step up and help us all achieve more with GenAI.

 

Tchau for now!

 

Leo

entry.title: NF-e Technical Note 2024.001 1.10 Version: Recent Updates on Nota Fiscal Eletrônica
entry.content:
(Versão em português aqui) 

Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound and outbound invoicing options for Brazil team has released updates on the Nota Fiscal Eletrônica (NF-e).

The update complies with SEFAZ NF-e Technical Note 2024.001 1.10 Version. The new version is planned to be in production on September 2, 2024, according to the tax authority.

NF-e Technical Note 2024.001 1.10 Version

The new updates on the electronic notas fiscais (model 55) replace the denial process with the rejection process and add the option 4 (Simples Nacional - Microempreendedor Individual - MEI) in the Tax Regimen Code (Código de Regime Tributário) field.

You can implement these changes in the outbound invoicing option for Brazil by following the instructions on the SAP Note 3487670 and the SAP Note 3488179.

If you use inbound invoicing option for Brazil, follow the instructions on the SAP Note 3488179.

You can also implement the above SAP Notes in the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP NFE) solution.

Related blog posts:

  • Usage Analytics Dashboard
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Inbound Nota Fiscal

For a step-by-step guide on how to use the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound and outbound invoicing option for Brazil, refer to the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, inbound invoicing option for Brazil

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-IN
  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “like” (kudos), share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content.

You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago

entry.title: NF-e Nota Técnica 2024.001 Versão 1.10: Atualizações recentes na Nota Fiscal Eletrônica (NF-e)
entry.content:
(English version here) 

Olá pessoal,

O time de SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil entregou atualizações relacionadas à Nota Fiscal Eletrônica (NF-e). As atualizações estão conforme a Nota Técnica 2024.001 versão 1.10 de NF-e. A nova versão está prevista para entrar em produção em 2 de setembro de 2024, segundo a SEFAZ.

NF-e Nota Técnica 2024.001 Versão 1.10

As novas atualizações para notas fiscais eletrônicas (modelo 55) substituem o processo de denegação pelo processo de rejeição e adicionam a opção 4 (Simples Nacional - Microempreendedor Individual - MEI) no campo Código de Regime Tributário.

Você pode implementar essas alterações na opção de emissão de fatura de saída para o Brasil seguindo as instruções da SAP Note 3487670 e SAP Note 3488179.

Se você usa a opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil, siga as instruções na SAP Note 3488179.

Você também pode implementar as SAP Notes acima na solução SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP NFE).

Blog posts relacionados:

  • Dashboard do Usage Analytics
  • Como navegar facilmente nas páginas do SAP Help Portal para Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • Como navegar facilmente nas páginas do SAP Help Portal para Inbound Nota Fiscal

Para obter um passo a passo sobre como usar o SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de emissão de fatura de entrada e de faturamento de saída para o Brasil, consulte as seguintes páginas do portal SAP Help:

  • Opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil
  • Opção de emissão de fatura de entrada para o Brasil

Você pode receber alerta por email quando novas SAP Notes forem lançadas ou atualizadas nos componentes listados abaixo; basta seguir as instruções do SAP Knowledge Base Article 2171560.

  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-IN
  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR
  • LOD-LH-NFE

Acesse o gerenciador de modificações legais para ter uma noção do que está planejado para ser suportado. Consulte o SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 e a página Regulatory Change Manager para entender como usar esta ferramenta.

Gostou desse post? Dê um “like” (kudos) e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. Sinta-se à vontade para deixar suas perguntas no SAP Community e ler posts semelhantes no blogging space do SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Para acompanhar as últimas notícias da NF-e, siga a tag SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) e a comunidade SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Obrigado e até a próxima,
Thiago

entry.title: SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2024 (issue #29)
entry.content:
This is our first issue since SAP Sapphire, so we’ll feature some of the innovations announced at that event. AI was everywhere, with the goal of making users more productive in solutions across SAP’s portfolio. SAP Build Code is great example of a product that integrates AI to make application development faster and easier.

We are hosting our second SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago on August 13. Please join us to see the latest innovations in our portfolio, including how AI makes building SAP Fiori apps easier and a sneak preview of some planned functionality to make business users more efficient when using SAP Fiori apps.

If you want to meet with fellow developers and the SAP engineers who build our professional development tools, I encourage you to join our monthly SAP Fiori elements and SAP Fiori tools roundtable group.
Our next meeting is August 14, 2024.
Learn about the group at https://blogs.sap.com/2021/07/31/peer-to-peer-learning-at-the-sap-fiori-elements-and-sap-fiori-tools-roundtable/
You can also email me if you would like an invitation.

If you prefer to receive this in your inbox, please subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter.

Development News

Sapphire 2024 summary from a user experience perspective

Every session I attended included some discussion on how artificial intelligence will make the product experience better. This summary focuses on how to implement the SAP Fiori user experience as customers transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-sapphire-2024-highlights-on-ux-application-development-and-ai/ba-p/13723602

 

What’s new for application development, automation and integration on SAP BTP

SAP Build Code and BTP were everywhere at Sapphire, especially in the context of building applications that follow clean core principles.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-sapphire-2024-what-s-new-for-application-development-automation-and/ba-p/13736396

 

Streamlining data entry: exploring the new 'paste' feature in SAP Fiori elements V4 tables

The recent release of SAP Fiori elements for OData V4 introduces the ability to paste data into tables, much like you would in Microsoft Excel. This enhancement significantly elevates the user experience for Fiori elements V4 application users.

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/streamlining-data-entry-exploring-the-new-paste-feature-in-fiori-elements/ba-p/13710973

 

SAP Fiori tools adds tighter integration with ABAP Development Tools

Developers can now have a more integrated experience when working with ADT at the backend and SAP Fiori tools for front-end development You don’t need to manually switch between the tooling environments and remember which system and entity they wish to use. Now, with one click in ADT, you can seamlessly switch from ABAP to SAP Fiori development.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-fiori-tools-may-2024-release-adds-closer-integration-with-abap/ba-p/13720761

 

SAP Fiori tools 2406 includes added support for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and more

Developers can now seamlessly create and deploy applications to SAP S/4HANA Cloud directly from Visual Studio Code.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-fiori-tools-2406-update-includes-added-support-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13743708

 

Copy and paste like a pro with SAPUI5 tables

Maximizing the efficiency of business users through advanced Copy & Paste: SAPUI5 1.124 improves copy/paste operations in tables, letting business users work in a more natural way with improved productivity.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sapui5-tables-maximizing-efficiency-through-advanced-copy-amp-paste/ba-p/13723158

 

Create an app variant to introduce functionality without changing the original app

You can extend SAPUI5 applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP BTP, ABAP Environment with an application variant, without changing the original application.

https://help.sap.com/docs/bas/developing-sap-fiori-app-in-sap-business-application-studio/extending-sap-fiori-application-for-s-4hana-cloud

 

Announcing UI5 Web Components 2.0

UI5 Web Components 2.0 enhances performance, reliability, and sets the stage for future innovations for web development.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/blast-off-ui5-web-components-2-0-is-now-live/ba-p/13744227

 

Reality check on applying clean code principles in SAPUI5 development

Applying clean code principles to SAPUI5 development is essential for building maintainable, scalable, and robust applications. Real-world constraints can make it challenging to follow these principles perfectly. By understanding the constraints and striving for continuous improvement, developers can still achieve a high standard of code quality.

https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs-about-sap-websites/applying-clean-code-principles-in-sap-ui5-development-reality-check/ba-p/13739199

 

Product updates for SAP Business Application Studio – June edition

As we cross the halfway mark of the year, it’s the perfect time to reflect on our progress and introduce the exciting new features we've added to BAS over the past few months.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/product-updates-for-sap-business-application-studio-june-edition/ba-p/13721825

Events

SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago – August 13

After our successful event in Dublin. Ireland, we are hosting another SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago. By attending this event, you will learn:

  • SAP’s UX strategy and how it is evolving
  • Recent UX innovations that fulfill the promise of the intelligent, collaborative enterprise
  • Making SAP Fiori work for you – finding the apps you need and adapting them to your business requirements
  • Building apps using the latest tools and technologies – the same ones SAP uses to build apps for SAP S/4HANA (including optional hands-on workshop)
  • How to move your organization forward, including how to take advantage of AI for developing applications

To bring you this content at no charge, this event is sponsored by Mindset Consulting, Convergent IS, and Sovanta.

Register today: https://events.sap.com/noam/sap-fiori-innovation-day-chicago-2024/en/home

Watch the recordings from re>≡CAP 2024 and UI5con 2024

Missed our community conferences around our programming frameworks CAP and SAPUI5? No worries! All sessions were recorded and are now available online.
Go to re>≡CAP event page to access recordings
Go to UI5con event page to access recordings

Tutorials

openSAP content has moved to the learning.sap.com platform

Developing and Extending SAP Fiori Elements Apps

https://learning.sap.com/courses/developing-and-extending-sap-fiori-elements-apps

Developing an SAP Fiori Elements App Based on a CAP OData V4 Service

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-an-sap-fiori-elements-app-based-on-a-cap-odata-v4-service

Adapting the UI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Using SAP Screen Personas

https://learning.sap.com/courses/adapting-the-ui-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-using-sap-screen-personas

 

Tutorial: Key User Adaptation for SAPUI5 applications

To extend SAPUI5 applications, SAPUI5 Flexibility offers capabilities not only for developers, but also for key users and end users using simple visual / WYSIWYG tools..

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/new-tutorial-mission-key-user-adaptation-for-sapui5-applications/ba-p/13639694

 

Tutorial: SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio are not new. What is new, is the support for SAP S/HANA Cloud. This enables developers to enhance SAPUI5 applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with features that were previously only for on-premise customers. See this blog post linking to an extensive, end-to-end tutorial:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/extending-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-using-sapui5-adaptation-projects/ba-p/13607813

Back issues from the past year

SAP FIori development newsletter #28 (May 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #27 (March 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #26 (January 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #25 (November 2023)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #24 (September 2023)

entry.title: Statement of Direction - SAP Data Services as of July 2024
entry.content:
Statement of Direction – SAP Data Services

July 2024

Introduction

As our matured products continues to have consistent traction in the market with emerging technologies and data platforms, it is essential for us to focus on extending its versions to meet the evolving needs of our customers. This Statement of Direction outlines our strategic approach to extending product versions, ensuring that we maintain our competitive edge in the on-premises data integration space as well as on-cloud and deliver value to our customers. Our primary objective is to understand and address the specific requirements of our customers, for the matured products as well as new and emerging technologies in the cloud. We will actively engage with our user base through feedback sessions, support channels, and market research to identify their pain points, challenges, and emerging needs.

 

Executive Summary

This document provides guidance to our customers of SAP Data Management portfolio, with respect to SAP Data Services (DS) solution, on the product directions.  For Information Steward we will have a separate communication made available. This document is an extension of our  blog post made on November 3, 2023, focused on SAP Data Services

SAP Data Management portfolio expanded further in the cloud with the introduction of SAP Datasphere in March 2023. SAP Datasphere is a comprehensive data service built only on SAP® Business Technology platform (SAP BTP) that enables every data professional to deliver seamless and scalable access to mission critical business data. It is the next generation of SAP® Data Warehouse Cloud, with new capabilities that enhance data discovery, modeling, and distribution. With a unified experience for data integration, data cataloging, semantic modeling, data warehousing, data federation, and data virtualization.  SAP Datasphere enables data professionals to easily distribute authoritative business data with business context and logic preserved across the data landscape.

As we define the future of SAP Data Management portfolio, with our stated data management strategy, current on-premise SAP Data Services customers can in fact leverage all their existing investments and efforts, repurposing those time-tested and trusted artifacts and configurations that are being used today in SAP Data Services (DS), while also extending the reach of their data management to the cloud, with SAP Datasphere, a native cloud solution. Yes, we are committed to our existing SAP Data Services and customer base too. In the current release of SAP Data Services, we do have the connector to SAP Datasphere to consume the raw, semi-processed as well as fully processed data from SAP Data Services connections. This capability enables to reduce the gap of heterogeneous sources and targets between SAP Data Services and SAP Datasphere.

“In 2022 we did a minor version upgrade of SAP Data Services to 4.3, allowing the compatibility with Business Intelligence BusinessObjects 4.3. As a result, our support policy extended the end of mainstream maintenance timeline to end of 2025 and priority 1 support to end of 2027 for both SAP Data Services and SAP Information Steward. However, with the mainstream maintenance of version 4.3 of SAP Data Services suite ending in 2025, we do not want to leave our large customer base behind, and therefore we have decided to release an updated version, code named SAP Data Services 2025 to provide more time to our on-premises customers to adopt our rapidly evolving, state-of-the-art cloud data integration scenarios. As more of our customers shift their on-premises workloads to the cloud, we also offer SAP Data Services, private cloud edition (PCE), which runs the ETL and Data Quality solutions in a managed cloud environment operated by SAP. Many of our largest SAP Data Services customers already run both solutions side-by-side, during their transition to the managed cloud deployment.”

SAP Data Services 2025

Our current plan to release a new version of the SAP Data Services in 2025 as a successor of SAP Data Services 4.3 remains unchanged and will be...

  • code named SAP Data Services 2025.
  • available on-premises and through managed cloud,  
  • focused on the most widely adopted on-premises scenarios: SAP Data Services for native ETL and Data Quality, 

o   Existing architecture and component map will be followed including Designer, Repository, Job Server, supported web applications and BI module.

o   Compatible with proposed BI 2025 suite and planned to be released a quarter after BI 2025

o   We have plans to release the first version by the end of Q1 2025 followed by ‘Fix Packages’ as needed.

o   We have plans to release a new version every alternate year and reduce the frequency of SAP Data Services, (SP) Service Pack releases. The ‘Service Pack’ release has been planned to be deprecated.

o   We will provide clear use case migration paths/workarounds for the components for the version we plan the end of support beyond 2027. 

o   Continue to support Windows and Linux platforms only. AIX and Solaris support has already been discontinued in the 4.3 version of SAP Data Services.

o   SAP private cloud edition (PCE) will continue to be primary option of availability for SAP Data Services on managed cloud as well as on premise.  The option to host on customers choice of private cloud and AWS EC3, Azure VM etc. remains unchanged.

o   We do not foresee any impact to solutions and partners using SAP Data Services in an OEM mode.   

o   We also have plans to discontinue the support of few sources and targets based on their sunset timelines, market demand and popularity score.

 

 We are committed to support you.

We recognize the importance of continuously improving and innovating our product and yet manage a balance between our on-premises and cloud-based solutions. We will invest in research and development to explore new technologies, industry trends, and market demands yet support our existing customer base for the mature products. By fostering a culture of innovation and collaboration, we will ensure that our product versions remain cutting-edge and relevant in the ever-changing business landscape. We are committed to help and support our customers.

Many customers rely on SAP Data Services for critical use cases and will need to do so beyond 2025. Our support plan for SAP Data Services is as follows and our message is consistent. Please refer to the respective GA versions Product Availability Matrix for the support timelines.

Your choice of deployment

With SAP Data Services, private cloud edition (PCE), we offer a predefined package operated by SAP on the main cloud platform providers, including software, support, technical managed services, and infrastructure. This also, opens an opportunity to shift from a maintenance contract to a subscription model while leveraging your existing investments in SAP Data Services solution.

Here are some of the key benefits:

  • The latest version of SAP Data Services is always available, thanks to the periodical upgrades.
  • 3-tier landscape: development/test, quality assurance and production by default, with optional additional tiers.
  • Pre-configured hardware landscapes optimized for performance,
  • Choice of cloud platform providers infrastructure includes AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Microsoft Azure.
  • End-to-end SLA 99.7%, backup and restore included within standard service scope.

In the future, customers will continue to have the choice for their infrastructure for SAP Data Services solution, either on-premises or in a managed cloud operated by SAP. Additionally, our directions for the on-premises data integration and data governance scope with respect to SAP Data Services and SAP Information Steward remains mutually exclusive and unchanged due to the data integration and data governance scope within SAP’s cloud solutions.

We have plans to stop or reduce the scope of few functionalities. 

  1. PeopleTools  for PeopleSoft Applications
  2. JD Edwards Application connectivity
  3. SAP Sybase Replication Server
  4. Siebel
  5. SalesForce Adapter
  6. Attunity aka Qlik Data Integration Platform

 

WE WANT TO HEAR FROM YOU

SAP Data Services remains as a key component of our Data Management portfolio, and we are committed to our customers. Please help us fine tune our roadmaps of SAP Data Services providing your input through the dedicated inbox:

SAP Data Services:               sap.dataservices@sap.com

We appreciate your feedback and will answer your questions via our official communication channels.

entry.title: Innovations Highlights from SAP Business Technology Platform - Sapphire 2024
entry.content:
Sapphire is always a highlight. But this year it was a special Sapphire. With this year´s event theme "Bring Out Your Best" our latest business AI innovations, products, and partnerships were highlighted. Take a look at this blog post where I want to focus on the most important announcements of SAP BTP at Sapphire 2024 (also non-AI related). In addition let´s explore innovation highlights coming up in the future. Enjoy reading on.

Clean Core Strategy with New SAP Build and ABAP Cloud Features

Following a clean core strategy means keeping your systems as unmodified as possible and creating your custom code in a way that does not effect upgrades and adoption of the latest innovations. But there is still the need to tailor your systems to your own businesses. To achieve this, we recommend that you go for side-by-side extensibility scenarios with SAP BTP, meaning that you build your own extensions to SAP systems on SAP BTP, whenever possible. SAP BTP offers three main options for this scenario: SAP Build low-code solutions, SAP Build Code and SAP BTP ABAP Environment.

If we look at ABAP, the first highlight is the Joule´s ABAP Developer capabilities, which will contain multiple features all tailored to increase developer efficiency. It will be the GitHub Copilot for the ABAP world embedded directly into the ABAP Development Tools in Eclipse (ADT). You will receive functionality embedded into wizards and an integrated chat. Planned GenAI features are explain ABAP code and CDS code, test and test data generation for ABAP and CDS, ABAP code completion, and many more. 

 

To simplify this process, we are building a way to access SAP Build directly from S/4HANA. Providing the ability to access SAP Build directly and with context from SAP S/4HANA, your development teams and their business stakeholders will be able to create new processes and process extensions much faster and more convenient than in the past. 

 

 

Guided experiences in SAP Build Work Zone

In addition the Guided experiences in SAP Build Work Zone let developers create step-by-step line-of-business workflows. This simplifies complex business processes through automation without custom code. With the Guided experiences, you can empower your workforce with seamless digital experiences tailored to their needs, boosting user satisfaction, engagement, and retention. You can automate tasks and streamline processes, allowing employees to focus on high-impact work.

Accelerate enterprise automation with AI

We are also enriching our Process Automation offering with Joule. Our GenAI-based copilot will help you to simply specify in natural language what kind of workflow or automation you would like to have. In addition our tools, with the help of different large language models, will help you either source these artifacts from available content, or they will generate new process and automation content for them using the power of AI.

Accelerate integration with AI

We believe that by embedding AI into SAP Integration Suite, you will be able to shorten project times and integrate applications and systems faster by using AI generated mappings, scripts, and integration flows without even needing to be a high end integration expert. The SAP Integration Suite will improve the quality of integration flows and business processes by helping you identify pre-built content packages or by supporting AI-generated artifacts that fit to your specific requirements. Same as with code or process automation artefacts, you will be able to generate integration artifacts, our so called integration flows, by leveraging generative AI and the Unified Customer Landscape service which provides your specific landscape details as context for the integration.

Anomaly detection with SAP Integration Suite

We are making use of machine-learning based capabilities in SAP Integration Suite to offer API anomaly detection. What this means is that we use Machine Learning to identify issues in your landscapes. Think of a sudden surge or drop in API calls, an increase in latency or an increase in the number of errors detected. We can identify this kind of unusual behavior and actively alert your stuff to these anomalies in API performance. This way, we can help identify and address issues before they escalate, minimizing disruptions and optimizing system performance. 

 

 

Simplified transition

How do I transition from SAP PI / PO to SAP Integration Suite? We know that many of you relying on PI/PO for the daily business and we want to make sure we safeguard the investments you have made. Therefore we will provide a simplified transition from SAP PO to SAP Integration Suite with features like mass migration, reduction of migration templates, and reduction of JMS queues using a pipeline concept. Also, there is a free assessment and planning services available where we provide customer specific recommendations on how to move your integration to the cloud.

If you want to learn more what´s new for Application Development, Automation and Integration on SAP BTP  read the blog post of @thomas_volmering.

Infuse AI in SAP Analytics Cloud

How do we evolve our portfolio in the space of Data and Analytics moving forward? First: We integrate Joule as your GenAI copilot in SAP Analytics Cloud. You can analyze, consume and create story content with Joule moving forward. You also have also the possibility to ask questions and get answers as charts. Or you can generate some scripts to make the story interaction for your end-users richer and more interactive. Remember the just ask feature? This will be integrated with Joule as well. The key thing to understand is that while the just ask feature is providing natural language query capabilities, Joule will be using this feature as well, but provide a more conversational experience in remembering the conversation and the user progress and integrations.

Extended capabilities in SAP Analytics Cloud

One more thing on Data and Analytics, as it touches both the themes of openness and connectivity. Later this year it is planned that SAP Analytics Cloud content will be able to natively be integrated with Microsoft PowerPoint. Using our add-in, you can intuitively connect to an SAP Analytics Cloud tenant and find user-created content to integrate into your presentation. For example stories, in which business users communicate business information using a combination of data and visual elements (like, building a heat map of North America illustrating where a company’s sales are overperforming and underperforming commercial expectations). Or Widgets, which are visual elements used to tell stories (for example, charts, tables, geographical maps, or buttons).

SAP Analytics Cloud will also let you connect to SQL data sources as live data connections. This eliminates the need for direct data replication, resulting in much faster access to real-time data, reduced data storage requirements, more reliable data consistency, improved system performance and lower costs.

More to come...

For me personally it has been a quite impressive and innovative Sapphire 2024. If you want to explore more highlights coming up in 2024 watch the replay of my session "Explore the road map of SAP BTP".

You did not make it in person or virtually? No worries, you still have the chance to watch the replays and especially the Opening Keynote of Christian Klein and Julia White. Explore the SAP Sapphire News Guide 2024  to learn more about the announcements.
Interested in more innovation highlights coming up? Then take a look at our Road Map Explorer. We do have more than 1000 innovation highlights planned for SAP BTP 2024.

Also be informed that this blog and SAP's strategy and possible future developments, products and or platforms directions and functionality are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice.

entry.title: GenAI Reference Solution Architecture on SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Introduction

In a remarkably short span, GenAI has taken the world by storm. With the help of Large Language Models (LLMs) we can tap boundless opportunities for everyone. Enterprises globally can harness the power of GenAI to innovate and streamline their processes like never before.

SAP is at the forefront of this revolution, making substantial investments in GenAI. With its robust, enterprise-ready Business Technology Platform (BTP), it enables the creation of GenAI solutions that seamlessly integrate with both SAP and non-SAP cloud services and customer's S/4 HANA ERP system.

In this blog, I present a solution architecture for a side-by-side extension that not only integrates seamlessly with S/4 HANA but also leverages the cutting-edge Generative AI services on the Business Technology Platform. Discover how this powerful combination can transform your enterprise, driving efficiency and unlocking new possibilities for growth and innovation.

I will structure this blog in 2 sections.

  1. Architectural Blueprint: A detailed architecture diagram illustrating the design.
  2. Component Breakdown: In-depth explanations of each component, highlighting it's importance and benefits in the architecture.

Let's get started. 

 

Architectural Blueprint

The following diagram presents an architectural blueprint for GenAI solution or S/4 HANA extension, developed and deployed on BTP. This architecture ensures seamless integration with on-premise S/4 HANA through connectivity service and leverages hyperscaler LLMs via the Generative AI Hub to unlock AI capabilities. 

Architecture Diagram

 

Let's deep dive into each component.

 

Component Breakdown

In this section, we'll take an in-depth look at each component. Understanding these components and their responsibility will provide a comprehensive view of how they collectively contribute to the solution.

Business Technology Platform (BTP):

This is the platform for creating an Intelligent Enterprise. It is an integrated offering comprising of database, application development, integration and intelligent technologies like GenAI. As you can see in the architecture diagram, you can create and integrate your solution completely leveraging cloud services on BTP. As you can see some of the many services leveraged in the architecture as listed below

  1. HANA Cloud database
  2. Generative AI Hub
  3. Cloud Foundry Runtime
  4. SAP Build Work Zone
  5. Connectivity service

We will be going in details of each one of it in the following sections.

HANA Cloud Database:

SAP HANA Cloud is SAP’s database-as-a-service offering, giving you the power and performance of SAP HANA in the cloud. The SAP HANA Cloud allows you to consume the SAP HANA database from applications running on SAP Business Technology Platform.

SAP HANA Cloud vector engine provides capability to create relevant and responsible GenAI solutions. Recent advances in GenAI and Large Language Models has generated importance for a reliable vector databases. Similarity search complements relational database and full-text search systems. Embedding functions map natural language text to high-dimensional vectors. Since LLM data can lead to hallucinations, vector databases can complement LLM responses with additional relevant text through Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG). Leveraging vector database like SAP HANA Cloud can ensure response quality from LLM.

Generative AI Hub:

Generative AI Hub provides you access to diverse set of LLMs which can be integrated with your solution on SAP BTP in a cost effective way. It gives access to SAP foundational models and open source models. In the architecture diagram, I have considered Azure OpenAI LLMs which are described in brief below

  1. gpt-4o-turbo: Useful for generating relevant response for applications based on prompt and content. Assisting language translations and summarization.
  2. text-embeddings-ada-002: For creating embeddings for the content or summary. Used for clustering similar text together. The embeddings are stored in vector database which can be referred at later point of time.

Cloud Foundry Runtime:

Cloud foundry run time helps you develop and run cloud native solutions. It is built on open standards where developers can choose language of their choice. For example - Node.js, JAVA or Python.

You can create applications leveraging CAP (Cloud Application Programming) model and access underlying custom tables in HANA cloud database using declarative programming paradigm with CDS (Core Data Services). I have showcased a full stack CAP application deployed on cloud foundry runtime.

It also provides Python build packs for creating AI driven microservices. In the architecture diagram, you can see the Python microservice is integrating with LLMs using python libraries (generative-ai-hub-sdk & ai-core-sdk). Using opensource library like Langchain, you can seamlessly integrate with vector engine in SAP HANA Cloud. These python libraries are available to install from python packaging index (PyPi).

SAP Business Application Studio provides support for development for all the above mentioned languages to ease the development process.

SAP Build Work Zone: 

SAP Build Work Zone enables organizations to establish a centralized point of access to SA custom-built, and third party applications and extensions. As shown in the Architecture diagram, the free style UI5 CAP application is integrated in the SAP Build work zone, from where it can be accessed based on roles configured for the user.

Connectivity Service:

SAP BTP Connectivity service enables cloud applications deployed on BTP to securely access remote services on the Internet or on-premise. It allows configuration of application connections via destinations and facilitates connections to on-premise systems using the Cloud Connector. This component also establishes a secure tunnel with your on-premise network so your applications can communicate with on premise systems securely with trust.

As shown in the architecture diagram, CAP service integrates with S/4 HANA APIs (On Premise) securely through cloud connector which is configured on the customer premises. CAP service integrates with other cloud applications via destinations configured in the connectivity service.

 

Conclusion

To conclude, the integration of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) with advanced Generative AI (GenAI) and Large Language Models (LLMs) offer countless opportunities for enterprises to scale efficiently. By leveraging the robust capabilities of vector databases and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), businesses can significantly enhance the accuracy and quality of AI-driven solutions.

The architectural blueprint provided in this blog demonstrates how seamless integration with on-premise S/4 HANA systems and the utilization of hyperscaler LLMs through the Generative AI Hub can be achieved leveraging cloud services to create side by side extension on BTP thus following the "Clean Core" approach.

Note: The content in this blog is based on my experience working on SAP BTP and creating AI solutions on BTP.

To get more updates about this topic, please follow the below pages

  1. SAP Business Technology Platform 
  2. Generative AI Hub
  3. generative-ai-hub-sdk
  4. Learning Journeys

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me – avinash.vaidya@sap.com

entry.title: See how SAP Enterprise Support helps customers use SAP Build to empower their digital transformation
entry.content:
SAP Build is a comprehensive suite of low-code and pro-code tools designed to accelerate application development and automation with the power of generative AI. It is built on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) from SAP that helps accelerate development and automation. SAP Build is an enabler for everyone no matter the skill level to create and enhance enterprise applications, process automations, and business sites, all with drag-and-drop simplicity. 

Consisting of three components SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Work Zone, it empowers more users to develop irrespective of their skill level thereby lessening the burden on professional developers.

SAP Build Code is a comprehensive application development environment tailored for developers working with Java and JavaScript. The turnkey solution combines runtime and design time capabilities with built-in enterprise solution integrations, DevOps, and Application Lifecycle Management. Joule, the AI copilot that understands your business, is available to assist with code generation.

In this blog, we are elaborating the assets which are available to provide foundational Support to customers as part of their SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition subscription to use the SAP Build portfolio to empower their digital transformation. 

With SAP Enterprise Support, customers can:

  • Receive guidance and realize value through AI-powered services, best practice content, remote quality checks, and assessment services. 
  • Empower your team to gain knowledge and skills and collaborate with experts for answers to support-related questions.
  • Transform your end-to-end landscape with application lifecycle management solutions to deploy, operate, and optimize cloud and hybrid business solutions.

If you want to just gain an overview of the SAP Build portfolio we have provided this SAP Build Big Picture session. In this e-learning, you will gain an overview of the SAP Build portfolio including SAP Build Code. It discusses the importance of the Clean Core concept and explore the low code/no code tools available for building new applications. Additionally, a high-level overview of SAP Build Code application development, assisted by Joule, is provided. For more detail, we have provided all the overview sessions for the SAP Build portfolio in the learning program Learn to develop using low-code through SAP Build provided through the SAP Enterprise Support Value Map program.

Below we have listed the SAP Build components and the corresponding support provided to customers to get started:

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Process Automation powers the automation of workflows and tasks with comprehensive workflow management, robotic process automation, and AI capabilities.  

To gain an overview to SAP Build Process Automation we have created a Discovery Center mission on Foundational support to start with SAP Build Process Automation. This includes an introductory session which has a demo the tool in action. Also it includes a multi-day Expert Guided Implementation service on SAP Build Process Automation. The purpose of this EGI service is to ramp-up customers to understand and adopt SAP Build Process Automation. In the first step, we give an overview of process automation in the context of SAP Build. Afterwards we show case the capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation and customers learn how to create workflows, decision management, and task automation with powerful yet intuitive no-code capabilities through the configuration of a specific business process to approve Sales Orders automatically. Further information including how to register for the service is provided here.

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps is an evolution of SAP AppGyver and it enables users to build web and mobile enterprise applications.  With hundreds of templates and pre-built business logic components, you can jumpstart your application projects and reduce development time. 

To gain an overview to SAP Build Process Automation we have created a Discovery Center mission on Foundational support to start with SAP Build Apps. This includes an introductory session. Also it includes a multi-day Expert Guided Implementation service on SAP Build Apps. The purpose of this EGI service is to ramp-up customers to understand and adopt SAP Build Apps. In the first step, we give an overview of application development in the context of SAP Build. Afterwards we show case the capabilities of SAP Build Apps and customers learn how to create mobile and web applications, connect to SAP systems, and deploy and run on SAP Business Technology Platform.​ Further information including how to register for the service is provided here.

SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Work Zone, brings the capabilities of SAP Launchpad and SAP Work Zone into one solution, facilitating the rapid creation of engaging and personalized business sites. It provides centralized access to business application information on any device.

To give an overview on SAP Build Work Zone, we have created the e-learning Explore the power of SAP Build Work Zone - SAP BTP Adoption Tutorial which provides a comprehensive Central Entry Point Digital experience. It encompasses an introduction to essential tools such as SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Task Center, and SAP Mobile Start. Key topics are discussed including the capabilities of SAP Build Work Zone, both standard and advanced editions and Identity and Access management through the SAP Cloud Identity Service. Also, we take a deep dive into the One Inbox suite quality of the SAP Task Center. and SAP Mobile Start as your native mobile entry point.

To provide further information for SAP Build Work Zone, we have created additional e-learnings. Firstly, we give an overview to integrate SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and SAP Mobile Start. We walk you through the configuration process for the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition ensuring you gain a comprehensive understanding of its setup and functionalities. Also, you will gain proficiency in integrating SAP Mobile Start with SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.

Also, we have provided an e-learning for SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition Deep Dive to effectively configure, deploy, and manage SAP Build Work Zone environments, leveraging its capabilities to enhance collaboration and productivity within your organization. The content shows how to set up SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition with integration of IAS (Identity Authentication Service) and IPS (Identity Provisioning Service) for user access, the process of creating a workspace and adding content such as How-to documents and technical support access, developing a UI Card in SAP Business Application Studio and deploying it to the workspace and integrating the Launchpad service and adding exposed content from an SAP S/4HANA system.

SAP Build Code

Apart from the SAP Build Big Picture session which provides an overview to SAP Build Code, we are piloting a new Expert Guided Implementation service on Cloud Application Programming Development using SAP Build Code. The purpose of this service is to empower customers with the knowledge of CAP development in SAP HANA Cloud and provide customers with the knowledge to develop and design in SAP HANA Cloud utilizing the SAP Business Application studio using SAP Build Code and the SAP AI co-pilot Joule. To find out more about this pilot through SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council, please reach out directly.

The e-learning Clean Core with SAP Business Technology Platform offers valuable insights to understand how SAP Business Technology Platform can help cultivate a cleaner and more sustainable core for SAP systems. It offers insights into how SAP BTP can support the fundamental pillars of data, integration, and extensibility. It explores the topic of extensibility in-depth and how the various components of SAP Business Technology Platform support the building of extensions in line with clean core principles.

For customers, if you have issues accessing the above links, there may be an authorizations issue. Please note a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required for most of the above assets. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

For internal colleagues, if you cannot access the above links, please use the following link, click on “Access” and use the above direct link once access has been granted.

We hope that this range of content and assets will support you in your digital transformation. For any queries in relation to any of the above assets, please reach out to us directly.

Thanks and Regards,

Robert Kelly

SAP BTP Lead Architect – SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q2-24 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
It has been a busy last quarter for all of us in SAP Sustainability! Besides delivering innovative new features and functions in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, we also had a strong presence at SAP Sapphire and have exciting success stories to share. This blog post continues my quarterly series and gives you an overview of some of the highlights. I’m covering:

  • Corporate Carbon Footprint Calculation
  • Corporate Balance Analytics
  • Emission Factor Mapping with AI
  • Spend-based Calculation for Scope 3.1
  • Other Release Highlights
  • SAP Sapphire 2024 Recap
  • Customer & Analyst Update

 

Corporate Carbon Footprint Calculation

In my last blog post (SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q1-24 Updates & Highlights), I already introduced our new form-based data collection approach, calculating Waste Generated in Operations. We have now enhanced this by Business Activities for any Greenhouse Gas (GHG) category, enabling you the calculation of a full Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) as per the GHG Protocol. You can set up those Business Activities at plant level by specifying the calculation method and emission factor for the footprint calculation. Business Users in your organization can then manually input activity data for these activities in the relevant apps. This data, combined with information from other sources, can be included in your overall footprint calculations and displayed in the analytical apps. This new approach provides you with maximum flexibility by enabling you to add data that isn’t available in your ERP system and to choose between activity- and spend-based calculations. It also speeds up the initial setup process and provides you with the possibility to start with the form-based data collection. You can then move to an ERP-centric collection over time for specific GHG scopes and categories, or also for specific plants in your company.

To set up the data collection, first ensure that the user roles are assigned correctly, and then configure the Footprint Inventory Scope. In the Manage Footprint Inventory Scopes app, edit an existing footprint inventory scope or create a new one, assign companies and plants, activate the appropriate GHG Data Form, and provide a guidance text for data entry. After you’ve done this, the form-based data collection will be enabled.

In the next step, you can create Business Activities for which you want to collect data. A Business Activity can be any activity in your company that you want to calculate emissions for, such as different types of travel (air, train, car) for various locations. Business users can then input relevant data (e.g., fuel used or distance traveled), and the system calculates emissions based on provided emission factors. Those can be updated over time by creating new emission factor periods within each activity. In the Manage Business Activities app, you can create a new activity by entering information such as the GHG Category and validity period. Here you also indicate the Preferred Footprint Type, defining which kind of data the business user later should input:

  • Manual Emission: Direct input of total CO2e values without needing to calculate or provide additional calculation data
  • Quantity-Based: Quantity data (e.g., material produced or distance traveled) to calculate CO2e
  • Spend-Based: Amount of money spent on an activity to determine CO2e

Finally, you need to specify the calculation method (e.g. distance- or fuel-based) and respective emission factor(s) by either entering it manually or selecting a reference from an LCA dataset. The system then calculates the total CO2e accordingly.

Manage Business Activities

After successful setup of the Business Activities, you can create GHG data collections. In the Manage GHG Data Collections app, those can be set up for each footprint inventory scope, period, plant, and business activity. The Data Collections can also be regenerated if there are changes in the Business Activities.

Now, the system is ready for the business users in your company to enter data in forms in the collection apps. In the Record GHG Data app, by default all relevant plants and periods are displayed. It's possible to exclude any unnecessary plants by marking them as Not Applicable. After selecting the desired plant, users can enter data – quantity, amount spend, or total CO2e – following the Preferred Footprint Type to ensure accuracy. Alternatively, data can be imported using the provided template. If the entered value differs by more than 30% from the previous period, the system flags this with a warning message.

GHG Data Form

In the final step, check data readiness and calculate footprints. In the Calculate Footprints app, you can as usual trigger the footprint calculation. If you’re using only the form-based data collection in the selected inventory scope, there’s no need to enter additional data. The progress and completion status is visible in the Data Readiness tab.

For more information on the different steps, check the scenario guide Form-Based Data Collection for Business Activities on SAP Help.

For a detailed guidance on setting up the solution for calculating corporate carbon footprints, check our brand-new Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) Implementation Guide. This package includes sample data for a model company and an implementation book to do an end-to-end setup of SAP Sustainability Footprint Management for calculating the CCF of the example company. Note that the target audience is consultants and partners, and access is restricted, requiring a login via your SAP account.

 

Corporate Balance Analytics

For analyzing the calculated corporate carbon footprint and the corporate balance, we released a new Sankey chart to visualize the emissions across a reporting period. This chart helps you to understand the major contributors for the emissions. Identifying these hotspots enables you to define specific actions for reducing the emissions in those areas and by that supporting your company’s decarbonization journey. The chart can be accessed directly via the Corporate Balance app or by clicking on the respective card in the Footprint Overview app.

Corporate Balance

You might have seen such a multilevel Sankey chart when visualizing a company’s financial balance and the respective financial flows. In SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, the company’s total CO2e emissions are displayed at the center, on the left side you see the input emissions from the different GHG scopes and categories, and you can follow their flow through the company. On the right, the proportions of the carbon emissions distributed among your various product and non-product outputs are visualized. The widths of the respective flows are proportional to their quantity. You can select a specific item subtype in the Sankey chart to display the breakdown of the item subtype into all the individual items in the table below the chart. The table is sorted based on the value of the emissions in descending order to help you gain insights. For example, clicking on 3.1 – Purchased Goods and Services will show you the emissions for all your purchased (raw) materials.
